groupingRule: All are fruits except Carrot (vegetable)
All are land animals except Shark (lives in water)
All are musical instruments except Painting (visual art)
All are flowers except Oak (tree)
All are 2D shapes except Cube (3D shape)
All are planets except Moon (natural satellite)
All are tools except Pillow (bedding)
All are drinks except Bread (food)
All are colors except Sweet (taste)
All are furniture except Bicycle (vehicle)
All can fly except Penguin (flightless bird)
All are physical sports except Chess (board game)
All are clothing except Refrigerator (appliance)
All are months except Summer (season)
All are string or woodwind instruments except Trumpet (brass)
All are fish except Crab (crustacean)
All are planets except Sun (star)
All are metals except Diamond (gemstone)
All are insects except Spider (arachnid)
All are writing tools except Eraser (removes writing)
All are land mammals except Whale (marine mammal)
All are vegetables except Strawberry (fruit)
All can follow the word 'brain' (brainstorm, brainwave, brainfall) except Trust
All are nouns that name a substance or element except Cold (adjective/state)
All can follow the word 'book' (booklight, bookhouse, bookworm) except Rain
All are units of time except Light (electromagnetic radiation)
All are months of the year except Spring (season)
All are words with animal and non-animal meanings except Bark (tree/sound, not an animal)
All produce light from energy conversion except Oven (primarily produces heat)
All connect two places except Tower (stands in one place)
All are ways of speaking except Write (visual communication)
All are natural fabrics/materials except Nylon (synthetic)
All measure time except Ruler (measures length)
All are involuntary reflexes involving sudden air movement except Blink (eyelid movement)
All are found underwater except Kite (flies in the air)
All have keys or strings except Drum (percussion, struck surface)
All allow or redirect light to pass through except Wall (blocks light)
All are medical professionals except Teacher (education)
All are temporary reproductions that vanish when the source changes except Photograph (permanent capture)
All are worn on hands or feet except Ring (worn on a finger, but is jewelry not clothing)
All are seasonings or spices except Flour (baking ingredient, not a seasoning)
All are light and rise or float except Anchor (heavy, sinks)
All cut or slice except Hammer (strikes)
All are bodies of water with a horizontal surface except Waterfall (vertical flow)
All are things that can be 'broken' except Wave (waves crash or break, but 'break a wave' is not a common idiom like the others)
All are things that 'run' (river runs, nose runs, story runs, stocking runs, conversation runs) except Clock (a clock 'ticks' not 'runs' in common English)
All can be described as having a 'charge' or 'current' (electrical or metaphorical) except Couch (no common charge association)
All have reflective surfaces except Floor (typically not reflective)
All are needed for travel or transactions except Bookmark (used for reading)
All involve climbing or ascending step by step except Elevator (ascends without steps)
All are things that 'run out' or 'burn out' except Rope (rope frays or snaps, not burns out)
All are things that can be 'filled' except Fence (fences enclose but are not filled)
All are things that 'spread' except Paint (paint is applied or spread, but the others spread autonomously)
All are things that can be 'cracked' except Dawn (dawn breaks, not cracks)
All produce a loud sound except Pillow (soft, silent)
All are things that 'unlock' something (metaphorically or literally) except Map (a map guides but does not unlock)
All are things that 'grow' over time except Crystal (crystals form, but 'grow' is less common in everyday usage vs. the others)
All are things that can be 'followed' except Patience (patience is exercised, not followed)
All are things that can be 'wound' (wind up a spring, watch, tape, clock, joke) except Joke (you tell a joke, not wind it)
All are fragile or breakable objects/concepts except Rule (rules are broken metaphorically but are not fragile)
All are intangible or natural phenomena that settle over an area except Blanket (a physical textile object)
All are things that keep something grounded or stable except Kite (designed to fly away)
All are things that 'absorb' (sponge absorbs water, student absorbs knowledge, soil absorbs rain, mind absorbs ideas, paper absorbs ink) except Mirror (reflects, does not absorb)
All are things that can 'burst' or 'erupt' except Seed (seeds sprout, not burst)
All are things designed to catch or confuse except Bridge (connects, does not trap)
All are things that 'turn' or 'change' cyclically except Brick (static, unchanging)
